frameworks.name: arousal-based uncertainty signaling
frameworks.one_line: central arousal indexed by pupil tracks internal uncertainty during decisions

frameworks.name: decision confidence
frameworks.one_line: internal probability that a perceptual choice was correct given evidence

frameworks.name: reward prediction error
frameworks.one_line: deviation from expected reward modulated by decision uncertainty

frameworks.name: perceptual decision-making under uncertainty
frameworks.one_line: framework for choices made with uncertain sensory evidence

core_question: Do pupil-linked arousal responses reflect decision confidence before feedback and uncertainty-weighted prediction errors after feedback during perceptual choice?
key_assumptions: pupil diameter is a peripheral marker of central arousal
decision confidence is an internally estimated probability of being correct
prediction errors scale with reward expectation deviations
manipulations: decision difficulty of the perceptual task
monetary feedback delivered after a delay
key_findings: pupil responses negatively scaled with decision confidence prior to feedback
post-feedback pupil responses scaled with uncertainty-dependent prediction errors
decision difficulty and accuracy interacted in shaping pupil responses
interpretations: arousal system signals two distinct computational variables at different decision stages
pupil-linked arousal supports learning and decision-making by tracking internal uncertainty
